regions: Europe, the Middle East and Africa

supply_chain.dependencies: TSMC manufactured approximately 95% of wafers from contract manufacturers during fiscal year 2024. Majority of front-end wafer manufacturing outsourced to external foundries. Internal fabrication facilities for proprietary processes (FBAR filters, GaAs and InP lasers) located in U.S. and Singapore. Primary warehouse in Malaysia.
supply_chain.risks: Dependence on contract manufacturers may adversely affect ability to bring products to market. TSMC could prioritize capacity for other customers or reduce deliveries on short notice. Limited number of suppliers for critical materials. Supply chain disruption risks from natural disasters, geopolitical tensions, trade restrictions. Concentration of facilities in Pacific Rim with seismic and severe weather risks.

regulatory_risks: U.S. Export Administration Regulations and export controls
Trade restrictions and tariffs (U.S.-China trade tensions)
Government restrictions on technology exports to specific countries
Anti-competition regulations and enforcement (FTC investigations, Korean Fair Trade Commission investigation)
Environmental regulations (climate change, water, hazardous materials)
Product chemical content limitations and restricted substances (lead restrictions in EU, China, U.S.)
Government procurement regulations and compliance requirements
ESG-related laws and regulations (environmental, social, governance disclosure requirements)
GDPR and sectoral data protection regulations
Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) compliance

sanction_risks: U.S. government restrictions on exports to China and specific Chinese entities
Potential for Chinese government retaliatory actions
Risk of being added to restricted entity lists
Technology export prohibitions to specific countries
Decoupling of U.S. and China economies
China-Taiwan geopolitical tensions affecting supply chain
